---
title: "Scalable Unified Python Package for Subsea CV at Petrobras"
author: "Rodrigo Souza dos Santos"
format: gfm
---

# Final OKR: Scalable Unified Python Package for Subsea CV at Petrobras

## Objective

Develop and launch a scalable, unified Python package that abstracts access to AI/ML resources and tools for computer vision projects in the Subsea division, enabling efficient, distributed processing, experimentation, and management of large and continuously growing subsea media datasets (images, videos, reports). Success in these projects will serve as a model for broader adoption across Petrobras.

---

## Key Results

### 1. Design & Architecture

- Deliver an architectural blueprint detailing integration points for AWS, Azure, GCP, Databricks, and Petrobras-specific tools, optimized for distributed CV workflows and large-scale data.
- Define and document standard APIs for:
    - Secure, scalable data ingestion and access (video, image, report formats)
    - Experiment management (classification, segmentation, multi-task workflows)
    - Feature engineering and storage, designed for high-volume, high-throughput operations

### 2. Development & Integration

- Implement core modules and processing pipelines capable of distributed, parallel processing, demonstrated by successful handling of large, diverse subsea media datasets.
- Achieve at least 80% code coverage in unit tests for all modules.
- Integrate experiment tracking and comparison functionalities for CV models, supporting:
    - Versioning of datasets, models, and results
    - Automated logging of metrics and artifacts
- Develop feature engineering and storage modules with:
    - Efficient storage, retrieval, and indexing of large-scale media and metadata
    - Metadata management for traceability, searchability, and auditability

### 3. Usability & Adoption

- Release a beta version to all 4 current subsea CV projects for pilot testing and feedback.
- Achieve a minimum satisfaction score of 8/10 from pilot users regarding usability, integration, and performance in large-scale subsea workflows.
- Publish comprehensive documentation and example notebooks covering:
    - End-to-end subsea CV workflows (from data ingestion to model deployment)
    - Best practices for handling massive media datasets and experiment management

### 4. Governance & Sustainability

- Establish a governance model for package maintenance, including:
    - Contribution guidelines and code review process
    - Release management and versioning strategy
- Ensure compliance with Petrobras’ security, privacy, and data governance standards for all integrated resources and media.

### 5. Impact & Expansion

- Demonstrate at least 2 successful end-to-end subsea CV projects using the package, showcasing improved efficiency, scalability, and project outcomes with large-scale data.
- Plan and initiate onboarding for additional subsea projects, aiming for at least 6 projects using the package within 6 months of launch.
- Document lessons learned, technical challenges, and success stories to support expansion to other Petrobras divisions.

---

## How Each Key Result Will Be Measured

- **Design & Architecture:**  
  Delivery and approval of architectural blueprint and API documentation, validated by technical leads and sample implementations.
- **Development & Integration:**  
  Successful processing and analysis of large, diverse subsea media datasets; automated test reports showing ≥80% code coverage; integration and performance benchmarks.
- **Usability & Adoption:**  
  Beta deployed to all 4 projects; user feedback surveys/interviews with ≥8/10 satisfaction; documentation and notebooks published and accessed.
- **Governance & Sustainability:**  
  Published governance model and guidelines; security/compliance audit reports; audit logs for traceability.
- **Impact & Expansion:**  
  Completion of 2+ end-to-end projects with before/after metrics; onboarding records for 6+ projects; documented lessons learned and success stories.

---

# Explanation for Stakeholders

> This initiative aims to create a robust, scalable Python package that will serve as a unified interface for all AI/ML and computer vision activities in the Subsea division. The package will abstract the complexity of accessing and managing large volumes of subsea media data (images, videos, reports) across multiple cloud and on-premises platforms. By enabling distributed processing and standardized workflows, the package will:
>
> - **Accelerate project delivery** by simplifying data access and experiment management.
> - **Improve efficiency and reproducibility** in handling massive, continuously growing datasets.
> - **Foster collaboration** through shared tools, documentation, and best practices.
> - **Ensure compliance** with Petrobras’ security and governance standards.
> - **Lay the foundation for expansion** to other divisions, multiplying the impact across the company.
>
> The project will be piloted with four current subsea CV projects, with clear metrics for adoption, usability, and technical success. Lessons learned will guide future scaling and adoption.

---

# Presentation Plan for Stakeholders

1. **Introduction**
    - Context: Subsea data growth and AI/ML challenges
    - Vision: Unified, scalable, and secure CV package

2. **Strategic Importance**
    - Business value: Efficiency, innovation, compliance
    - Alignment with Petrobras’ digital transformation

3. **OKR Overview**
    - Objective and Key Results
    - How success will be measured

4. **Technical Approach**
    - High-level architecture (with diagram)
    - Distributed processing and scalability
    - Integration with existing tools and platforms

5. **Pilot and Adoption Plan**
    - Initial rollout to 4 subsea projects
    - Feedback and continuous improvement loop

6. **Governance and Sustainability**
    - Maintenance, contribution, and compliance

7. **Impact and Expansion**
    - Metrics for success
    - Roadmap for broader adoption

8. **Q&A and Next Steps**

---

# Draft Technical Specification for the Package

## 1. Overview

A Python package to abstract and unify access to AI/ML resources, tools, and data for subsea computer vision projects, supporting distributed processing and large-scale data management.

## 2. Core Features

- **Resource Abstraction:**  
  Unified APIs for AWS, Azure, GCP, Databricks, and Petrobras on-premises storage.
- **Data Ingestion:**  
  Secure, scalable ingestion of images, videos, and reports; support for batch and streaming.
- **Distributed Processing:**  
  Integration with Spark (Databricks), Dask, and cloud-native batch/streaming services for parallel processing.
- **Experiment Management:**  
  Tracking, comparison, and versioning of datasets, models, and results; automated logging of metrics and artifacts.
- **Feature Engineering:**  
  Modular pipelines for preprocessing, augmentation, annotation, and feature extraction from media and reports.
- **Storage & Retrieval:**  
  Efficient storage, retrieval, and indexing of large-scale media and metadata; metadata management for traceability.
- **Security & Compliance:**  
  Authentication, authorization, and audit logging; compliance with Petrobras standards.
- **Documentation & Usability:**  
  Comprehensive documentation, example notebooks, and best practices for subsea CV workflows.

## 3. Architecture

- **Ingestion Layer:** APIs and connectors for data sources
- **Processing Layer:** Distributed engines (Spark, Dask, etc.)
- **Feature Store:** Centralized storage for engineered features
- **Experiment Tracker:** Integrated with MLflow or similar tools
- **Governance Layer:** Security, compliance, and audit modules

## 4. Extensibility

- Modular design for easy integration of new tools, data types, and workflows.

## 5. Testing & Quality

- ≥80% code coverage in unit tests
- Automated integration and performance tests

## 6. Deployment

- Containerized for cloud and on-premises environments
- CI/CD pipelines for automated builds and releases

## 7. Documentation

- User guides, API references, and example workflows

---
